metric_explanations.silhouette: Measures how well-separated clusters are. Range is -1 to 1; higher is better.
metric_explanations.davies_bouldin: Measures average similarity between clusters. Lower is better.
metric_explanations.calinski_harabasz: Ratio of between-cluster separation to within-cluster compactness. Higher is better.
metric_explanations.adjusted_rand_index: Agreement between cluster assignments and true burnout quartiles (chance-adjusted). Higher is better; 1 is perfect.
metric_explanations.normalized_mutual_info: How much information clusters share with true burnout quartiles. Range is 0 to 1; higher is better.

figure_explanations.cluster_sizes.png: Bar chart of number of students per cluster; useful to spot highly imbalanced clusters.
figure_explanations.cluster_vs_burnout_heatmap.png: Heatmap crossing discovered clusters with true burnout quartiles; highlights where clusters align or mix quartiles.
figure_explanations.pca_clusters.png: 2D PCA projection colored by cluster label; gives a visual sense of separation/overlap in reduced space.
figure_explanations.pca_true_burnout.png: Same PCA projection colored by true burnout quartiles; compare against cluster-colored view to assess alignment.
figure_explanations.elbow_plot.png: Inertia vs k curve for k=2..10; look for an elbow where adding more clusters yields diminishing returns.
